original_question:
Triangle $\triangle ABC$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $AB = 6, AC = 4,$ and $\angle BAC = 90^\circ.$ Let $D$ be the reflection across $\overline{BC}$ of the centroid of $\triangle ABC. {}$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $1, 2, 3,$ and $r$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $A, B, C,$ and $D,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $r$ can be written as $\tfrac mn,$ where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $m+n.$

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Triangle $\triangle DEF$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $DE = 8, DF = 6,$ and $\angle EDF = 90^\circ.$ Let $G$ be the reflection across $\overline{EF}$ of the centroid of $\triangle DEF.$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $2, 3, 4,$ and $s$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $D, E, F,$ and $G,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $s$ can be written as $\tfrac{p}{q},$ where $p$ and $q$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $p+q.$

Triangle $\triangle XYZ$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $XY = 10, XZ = 5,$ and $\angle XZY = 90^\circ.$ Let $H$ be the reflection across $\overline{YZ}$ of the centroid of $\triangle XYZ.$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $1, 4, 5,$ and $t$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $X, Y, Z,$ and $H,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $t$ can be written as $\tfrac{r}{s},$ where $r$ and $s$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $r+s.$

Triangle $\triangle PQR$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $PQ = 7, PR = 5,$ and $\angle PQR = 90^\circ.$ Let $I$ be the reflection across $\overline{QR}$ of the centroid of $\triangle PQR.$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $3, 1, 2,$ and $u$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $P, Q, R,$ and $I,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $u$ can be written as $\tfrac{x}{y},$ where $x$ and $y$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $x+y.$

Triangle $\triangle STU$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $ST = 9, SU = 4,$ and $\angle SUT = 90^\circ.$ Let $K$ be the reflection across $\overline{TU}$ of the centroid of $\triangle STU.$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $2, 3, 5,$ and $v$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $S, T, U,$ and $K,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $v$ can be written as $\tfrac{a}{b},$ where $a$ and $b$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $a+b.$

Triangle $\triangle ABC$ lies in plane $\mathcal P$ with $AB = 12, AC = 5,$ and $\angle BAC = 90^\circ.$ Let $M$ be the reflection across $\overline{BC}$ of the centroid of $\triangle ABC.$ Four spheres, all on the same side of $\mathcal P,$ have radii $2, 3, 4,$ and $w$ and are tangent to $\mathcal P$ at points $A, B, C,$ and $M,$ respectively. The four spheres are also each tangent to a second plane $\mathcal T$ and are all on the same side of $\mathcal T.$ The value of $w$ can be written as $\tfrac{c}{d},$ where $c$ and $d$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $c+d.$
